oneLiner: A refreshing, light protein drink that upgrades your everyday routine — for people who want wellness without sacrifice.
positioningStatement: For wellness-focused, identity-driven people looking to make healthier, enjoyable choices throughout daily life, PEP is a premium, protein-powered lifestyle drink that seamlessly fits brunches, coworking, beach hangs, or any uplifting moment. Unlike typical gym protein, PEP delivers vitality and flavor—with a light, sociable feel and a beautiful, minimalist look.
audience: Young professionals seeking convenient wellness (brunch, coworking, picnics)
Health-conscious urbanites (20s/30s) with active, social lifestyles
People who want elevated, inclusive drinks without sacrificing joy or taste
occasions: Brunch or breakfast at a café
Social small gatherings (day or low-key events)

voice.tone: Friendly, modern, light-hearted and uplifting; never clinical or restrictive. Warm and confident.

visual.mood: minimal, pastel, warm yet sophisticated (frequent use of cream, peach, and soft yellow with deep green accents)
visual.photography: Bright daylight, natural scenes, inclusive groups in relaxed but aspirational settings. Always real, never stocky or 'posed fitness'. No low-light nightlife or 'pump' aesthetics.
visual.typography: Mix of elegant serif (Didot, especially for wordmark/headings), friendly sans-serif (Montserrat), and signature-style script (Brittany Signature) for warmth and personality.

inLineBrands.name: BLOOM
inLineBrands.why: Integrates prebiotic benefits with lively, fun, wellness-centric branding; appeals to health-focused consumers seeking an elevated, treat-like daily option.

inLineBrands.name: EVER TREE
inLineBrands.why: Promotes natural refreshment through minimalist, elegant design and real-ingredient cues—a role-model for positioning as a wellness-focused soft drink.

inLineBrands.name: OLIPOP
inLineBrands.why: Combines friendly, health-forward branding with a soft color palette and wellness-driven value, directly appealing to the same wellness-lifestyle audience.

inLineBrands.name: Poppi
inLineBrands.why: Uses vibrant, wellness-focused branding and lively visuals to signal a fun, daily health treat—paralleling PEP's role as an uplifting upgrade.

inLineBrands.name: Alive & Well
inLineBrands.why: Focused on a smoother, more approachable kombucha for everyday wellness, aligned with PEP's lifestyle upgrade mission.

inLineBrands.name: Recess
inLineBrands.why: Targets the relaxation and wellness as lifestyle market with pastel minimalism and stress-relieving positioning; clear overlap with PEP's mood and occasions.

inLineBrands.name: Ole cocktails
inLineBrands.why: Benchmarks premium, vibrant, inclusive beverage branding for modern lifestyle moments (though alcoholic, the tone and design are directly relevant).

inLineBrands.name: Ish
inLineBrands.why: Sets a standard for premium, authentic, inclusive lifestyle drinks (alcohol-free); design and positioning fit lifestyle upgrade perfectly.

positioningFitTypeMismatch.name: Yaya Kombucha
positioningFitTypeMismatch.whyBrandingFits: Rich, authentic, wellness-driven; great lifestyle vibe for health-centric, ritualistic occasions.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.typeMismatch: Stronger in kombucha/fermentation category; not a protein drink but shares lifestyle and wellness cues.

positioningFitTypeMismatch.name: Walker Brothers
positioningFitTypeMismatch.whyBrandingFits: Natural, well-designed, earthy; fits craft-lifestyle space well.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.typeMismatch: Botanical/kombucha format rather than protein, but similar wellness ritual appeal.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: HOLY
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Appeals to purely functional, vitamin-enriched hydration needs—lacks emotional, lifestyle-driven positioning. Brand identity is simple, more clinical.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: LMNT
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Strictly functional for hydration/electrolytes (sports, keto, biohacker focus); lacks lifestyle aspiration and warmth.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: NOCCO
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Aggressively gym/sports performance brand; tone and design are not aligned with everyday wellness/lifestyle upgrade.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: PROTOCHOL
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Hybrid protein/alcohol, leans unconventional/edgy, protein function; not a daily casual wellness upgrade.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: VITAMIN WELL
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Functional hydration with vitamin focus, not positioned as a modern lifestyle treat—overly rational, less emotional appeal.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Kin Euphorics
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: More social/mood-enhancement/party ritual than lifestyle wellness; designs are trendy but positioned for night or event settings.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Lyre's
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Premium, very spirit-focused for alcohol-free social settings; less about daytime routines or wellness, more about bar ritual.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Seedlip
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Targets non-alcoholic spirits as a bar/cocktail replacement—more for night, events, not for routine wellness occasions.

antiReferences.name: Lean Body / gym RTD
antiReferences.why: Gym-focused, masculine, performance-only, 'bro' protein drinks. Completely wrong lifestyle signal—too functional, and excludes target user.

antiReferences.name: drinkpep.com (US competitor)
antiReferences.why: Same name, but completely different product (energy/probiotic USA drink); risk of confusion. Different founder, branding, and positioning.

antiReferences.name: Generic sports/energy drinks
antiReferences.why: Visual cues (bold type, metallics, lightning bolts, big 'energy' callouts) are completely off for an aspirational daytime wellness brand.

brandFitRules: Must signal an aspirational, wellness-driven, and lighthearted lifestyle; never functional/gym/protein-bro-centric.
Always use minimal, clean, friendly layouts with soft/neutral or pastel colorways.
Primary brand fonts: Didot (serif, for wordmark/headline), Brittany Signature (script, for warmth and personal touch), Montserrat (sans, for body/labels/badges). No aggressive or industrial type.
Photography and illustration must evoke daylight, outdoors, communal or self-care moments; never harsh lighting, dark bar, or gym scenes.
Language and copy must be uplifting, accessible, and inclusive; never punishing, technical, or intimidating.
Packaging should remain uncluttered, premium, and warm; avoid clinical/medicinal, or bold/extreme fitness visual motifs.
Functional cues (protein callout, nutrition) should be clear but never dominate or overshadow the lifestyle story.
Absolutely avoid lookalike or copycat branding of drinkpep.com or any protein-bro energy drink.
